title: practical english usage - Part 46
content:
- When I got home, water was running down the kitchen walls.

I lived in London for ten years while I was a child.

- It happened while I was living in Eastbourne last year.

we  use the simple past. Compare: actions and situations. When  we  talk about longer, more permanent situations The  past progressive, like other progressive forms (►   3), is used for temporary 6 used    for   shorter,     temporary        actions      and   situations She said she believed Joe was dying.               -...) Some verbs are not often used in progressive forms (►  4).

5 non-progressive           verbs:    She    said     she    believed At the time when it happened, / was travelling to New York a lot.

‘ background’  for the main action.

However, the past progressive is possible if the repeated actions form a not When I was a child we walked to school, (     ... we were walking to school.) not / rang the bell six times, (     / iws rmgjmg-lhe-Ml-six times.) habitual past actions. The  simple past is usually used with this meaning.

The  past progressive is not the normal tense for talking about repeated or 4 not   used    for   repeated       actions Section Past   and   Perfect     Tenses          5 grammar •  46  past verb form with present or future meaning weren't they?

Do you remember that nice couple we met in Greece? They were German, I'm sorry we left Liverpool. It was such a nice place.

Are you deaf? I asked how old you were.

are still true and situations which  still exist.

If we  are talking about the past, we often use past tenses even for things which 4 past    focus    on   continuing        situations:      It was     such     a  nice     place I think it might rain soon.      Alice should be here soon.

Could you help me for a moment? Would you come this way, please?

and shall. (►  311.4) future reference; they are used as less direct, 'distanced' forms of can, may, will The  'past' modal forms could, might, would and should usually have present or 3  'past'   modals:     could,      might,       would,       should I was hoping we could have dinner together.

I was thinking about that idea of yours.

even less direct. (►  311.2) Past progressive forms (Z was thinking!wondering/hoping, etc) make sentences Did you want cream with your coffee, sir?

I thought you might like some flowers.

I wondered if you were free this evening.

formulae are I wondered, I thought, I hoped, did you want. (^311.1.) using past tenses. (For more about 'distancing' of this kind, ►    311.) Common We   can make questions, requests and offers less direct (and so more polite) by 2 distancing        in  questions,       requests,      etc:  I wondered           if.  ..

I wish (that) I had a better memory. (►  632.4) (►  566.3) Don't come and see me today - I'd rather (that) you came tomorrow.

Ten o'clock - it's time (that) you went home. (►   502) In a perfect world, you would be able to say exactly what you thought.

Would you follow me wherever I went?

Supposing we didn't go abroad next year?

supper-time, unless the train was delayed, of course.

can use verbs like I had, you went or I was wondering to talk about the present A  past tense does not always have a past meaning. I've travelled in Africa a lot. (= I know Africa.) (►  48.1). Compare: We   do not use the present perfect if we  are not thinking about the present At last! I've finished!  Have you done all the housework?
